tldr: The site is in a healthier starting position than most local clinics — it already ships llms.txt, llms-full.txt, MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD, FAQ schema on the homepage, and an AI-crawler-friendly robots.txt. That covers roughly the top quartile of table-stakes GEO hygiene.

Nothing from the Tier 1 punch-list has shipped yet. The two biggest gaps vs. April-2026 best practice remain authorship / E-E-A-T signals on team and service pages (no Physician schema, no visible 'medically reviewed by' block near the top of service pages, no sameAs links to the Osteopathy NZ register) and content coverage — there are still no condition-specific landing pages or blog posts.

Fresh April 2026 signals sharpen the priorities: Google's April 2026 core update explicitly pushes author-credentials blocks to the top of YMYL pages (not the bottom); AI Overviews now trigger on ~48% of all searches and ~88% of healthcare queries; ChatGPT only recommends ~1.2% of local businesses, with Reddit/UGC mentions now one of the strongest off-site citation levers; and Google has publicly stated it does not use llms.txt for AI features (llms.txt still useful for dev-tool crawlers, but a <link> reference in the HTML head is now marginal).
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page

gaps.title: No Physician / Person schema on team profiles
gaps.why: YMYL + April 2026 core update rewards qualified, machine-readable authorship.

gaps.title: No AggregateRating / Review schema
gaps.why: '4.9 on Google' is on the homepage but not marked up, so AI cannot quote it.

gaps.title: No per-service Service / MedicalTherapy / HealthcareService schema
gaps.why: Service pages inherit the site-wide block but emit no page-level schema.

gaps.title: No BreadcrumbList
gaps.why: AI engines use breadcrumbs to understand hierarchy; also unlocks SERP breadcrumbs.

gaps.title: FAQ schema only on homepage
gaps.why: Service-specific Q&As are the most citation-friendly structure in 2026.

gaps.title: No visible dateModified / last-updated
gaps.why: 65% of AI citations go to content published in the last year; freshness matters.

gaps.title: No condition-specific pages
gaps.why: No indexable URL for 'sciatica osteopath Christchurch' etc. — biggest content gap.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months; ongoing publishing keeps citable surface area alive.

gaps.title: Homepage H1 not keyword-aligned
gaps.why: 'Our Practitioners at Meridian Osteopathy' should be H2; H1 should describe primary intent.

gaps.title: No sameAs / social profile links
gaps.why: Empty social URLs in site.js; nothing for AI engines to disambiguate the entity with.

gaps.title: No MedicalWebPage / ProfilePage per team profile
gaps.why: Each practitioner URL should be a standalone retrievable schema node.

gaps.title: No llms.txt reference in HTML head
gaps.why: Google has now publicly said it does not use llms.txt; a <link> hint is marginal. Kept for dev-tool crawlers that do check, but deprioritised.

gaps.title: Image alt text could be richer
gaps.why: AI-image-grounded models benefit from descriptive, not titular, alt text.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Tinuiti Q1 2026: Reddit now dominates AI-citation growth across nine tracked categories; long-form (300+ word) community answers cited ~3× more.

gaps.title: No top-of-page 'medically reviewed by' byline on service pages
gaps.why: April 2026 core update explicitly moves the author-credentials block to near the top of YMYL pages, not the bottom.

gaps.title: No long-form structured deep-answer blocks
gaps.why: FAQ-length Q&As are the floor, not the ceiling — 300+ word structured answers with subheadings are 3× more likely to be cited by Perplexity/AI Overviews.

items.title: Add AggregateRating + Review schema
items.body: Nest AggregateRating and three Review nodes under MedicalBusiness in the base JSON-LD. Use the real 4.9 / N-reviews figure from Google; review text can come from home.testimonials. Add reviewCount and ratingValue fields to home.json.

items.title: Add Physician JSON-LD to each team profile
items.body: Emit Physician JSON-LD per practitioner with name, jobTitle, worksFor → #clinic, medicalSpecialty, hasCredential (from qualifications), image, url, and sameAs (Osteopathy NZ register URL). Add a visible 'Registered with …' line at the top of each profile.

items.title: Add per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema
items.body: Each service page should carry its own MedicalTherapy or Service node referencing the clinic @id, with hasOfferCatalog of specialties and mainEntityOfPage link.

items.title: Add FAQ schema on every service page
items.body: Move the FAQPage JSON-LD pattern onto each service page (three to five service-specific Q&As). Content probably already exists in the service intro + specialty blocks — extract it into a faq array per service.

items.title: Fix homepage H1
items.body: Change 'Our Practitioners at Meridian Osteopathy' (line 17) to describe the clinic's primary intent — e.g. 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch' — and demote the practitioners heading to H2.

items.title: Add BreadcrumbList schema + visible breadcrumbs
items.body: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD built from page.url segments. Show a visible breadcrumb strip above the hero on non-home pages.

items.title: Add 'Last updated' visible + dateModified in schema
items.body: Surface a Last-updated date on service and team pages. Expose dateModified in page-level schema. Drive it from a front-matter updated field, falling back to page.date.

items.title: Fill social URLs + emit sameAs
items.body: Fill empty social URLs in site.js (at least the Google Business Profile URL) and add them as sameAs inside the clinic JSON-LD.

items.title: Expand llms.txt
items.body: Add Conditions-we-treat, Pricing-&-ACC, and Common-questions sections to llms.txt. Keep under 1500 words — Perplexity and others truncate.

items.title: Create four condition-specific pages
items.body: Each page 800–1200 words, structured Q→A with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, internal-link to the relevant service page. Add a 'What we treat' nav section.

items.title: Add ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage on team layout
items.body: Make each practitioner URL a standalone retrievable schema node so AI engines can cite the individual page.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Feeds the freshness signal AI engines reward.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. NOT astroturf — long-game brand-mention strategy. Tinuiti Q1 2026 confirms Reddit now dominates AI-citation growth; long-form (300+ word) comments cited ~3× more.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old.

items.title: Add 'Medically reviewed by' byline near top of each service page
items.body: Above-the-fold block on each service page: 'Medically reviewed by {practitioner name}, {registration body + number}, {last-reviewed date}'. Link the name to the team profile. Expose the same relationship in page-level schema via reviewedBy → Physician @id. Directly addresses the April 2026 core update, which moved credentials blocks from the bottom of YMYL pages to near the top.

items.title: Add a 300+ word structured deep-answer block per service page
items.body: One long-form answer per service targeting the top AI query (e.g. 'Does osteopathy work for sciatica?'). Subheadings, a short direct-answer intro, bulleted mechanism/evidence, an ACC/NZ-context paragraph, clear next step. Pairs with (not replaces) the FAQ schema from item #4. Tinuiti / Perplexity data show 300+ word structured answers are cited ~3× more than FAQ-length Q&As.
